summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that a crafted 22-byte LZ4 frame reaches Lz4FrameDecoder and forces a 33,554,432-byte buffer allocation request before the malformed block is rejected.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should make Lz4FrameDecoder request a 33,554,432-byte buffer after receiving a 22-byte attacker-controlled frame, demonstrating how very small inputs can drive disproportionate memory allocation and denial-of-service pressure.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned Netty tag netty-4.2.11.Final, checked out commit c94a8180e749f694fb76963dd494bae17c31aff6, built image evohunt-advisory_9f612283-lz4_frame_decoder_resource_exhaustion, and produced PAYLOAD_BYTES=22, MATCHED_ALLOC_INITIAL_CAPACITY=33554432, MATCHED_ALLOC_MAX_CAPACITY=33554432, SERVER_EXCEPTION_CLASS=java.lang.IndexOutOfBoundsException, and [evohunt] verification passed.
verification_method: verify.sh first confirms that the mounted target checkout contains the vulnerable allocator call in Lz4FrameDecoder, then runs the harness and requires the proof lines PAYLOAD_BYTES=22, ALLOC_REQUEST_SEEN=true, MATCHED_ALLOC_INITIAL_CAPACITY=33554432, MATCHED_ALLOC_MAX_CAPACITY=33554432, and a server-side decoder exception before declaring success.
evidence_produced_by_run: The reproduction printed PAYLOAD_BYTES=22, showing that the attack traffic is only 22 bytes long.
The reproduction printed MATCHED_ALLOC_INITIAL_CAPACITY=33554432 and MATCHED_ALLOC_MAX_CAPACITY=33554432, proving that the decoder requested a 32 MiB buffer.

The reproduction printed SERVER_EXCEPTION_CLASS=java.lang.IndexOutOfBoundsException after the allocation attempt, showing the malformed frame was rejected only after the oversized allocation path was reached.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable ref, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
